results.label: DPA — well drafted, minimal issues expected
missingClauses: DPIA and Prior Consultation Assistance (GDPR Art. 28(3)(f))
Details of Processing (Annex I: subject matter, duration, nature, purpose, data categories, data subject categories)
positiveClauses: 3. Confidentiality — satisfies GDPR Article 28(3)(b) requirement for confidentiality commitments
5. Sub-processors — well-drafted with prior written authorisation, listed sub-processors, and 30-day advance notice of changes, meeting GDPR Article 28(2) requirements
6. Data Subject Rights — clear assistance obligation with a specific 5-business-day timeline
7. Breach Notification — 24-hour notification window is stricter than GDPR's 'without undue delay' standard and demonstrates strong commitment

issues: Status wrong: got "significant_issues", expected one of [clean, needs_work] — too_severe

Prohibition on Sale of PHI and Marketing Use
HIPAA Definitions Section (referencing 45 CFR Parts 160 and 164)

redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag missing HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards

results.label: DPA — CCPA context, California users
missingClauses: Consumer Rights Assistance — CPRA requires contractual commitment to assist Business with consumer rights requests (access, deletion, correction, opt-out)
Breach Notification — No provision requiring Service Provider to notify Business of security incidents or data breaches, required under Cal. Civ. Code §1798.82 and CCPA §1798.150
Sub-Processor/Subcontractor Restrictions — No clause restricting or governing downstream disclosure of personal information to sub-processors, required for service provider status under CPRA
Audit/Compliance Verification Rights — No right for Business to monitor or audit Service Provider's compliance, needed to demonstrate reasonable oversight under CPRA
CPRA Service Provider Certification — Missing the required contractual certification that Service Provider understands and will comply with CPRA restrictions (§1798.140(ag)(1))
Data Retention/Minimization Policy — No limits on how long personal information may be retained or requirement to minimize data collected
Governing Law and Jurisdiction — No governing law clause despite processing California consumer data
positiveClauses: No-Sale Commitment (partial) — The agreement does include a prohibition on sale, though it needs to be expanded to cover sharing

issues: Missing flags: deletion request
Red flags: Does not flag that using consumer data to improve own models may constitute 'selling' under CCPA | Does not flag missing consumer rights obligations
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag that using consumer data to improve own models may constitute 'selling' under CCPA
Does not flag missing consumer rights obligations
Does not cite CCPA/CPRA for California consumer data
